pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 26 creates the requirements file at this exact path
setup_task.sh line 22 removes this path, export_result.sh line 9 reads it
setup_task.sh line 23 removes this path, export_result.sh line 10 reads it
export_result.sh lines 45 and 117-118 check for iot_hub component
export_result.sh lines 46 and 120-121 check for stream_analytics component
export_result.sh lines 47 and 123-124 check for cosmos_db component
export_result.sh lines 48 and 126-127 check for data_lake component
export_result.sh lines 49 and 129-130 check for power_bi component

privileged_info_summary: The azure_iot_telemetry_pipeline task is a synthetic task that requires users to create an Azure IoT architecture diagram in draw.io. The task uses synthetic requirements data generated by setup_task.sh at /home/ga/Desktop/iot_requirements.txt describing a Smart City IoT Telemetry Pipeline for Urban Temperature Monitoring. The diagram must include five specific Azure components: IoT Hub, Stream Analytics, Cosmos DB, Data Lake Storage, and Power BI, arranged in a hot/cold path architecture. Output files are saved to /home/ga/Desktop/smart_city_iot.drawio (source diagram) and /home/ga/Desktop/smart_city_iot.png (exported image). The task uses draw.io desktop application version 26.0.9 and relies on the Azure shape library for proper component representation.
